summaryTemplate: {{className}} is the {{patternRole}}. Observers register with a subject and are notified through a virtual callback whenever the subject's state changes, so the subject never needs to know what each observer does. Pattern body lives at {{file}}:{{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}.
chosenRationaleTemplate: Tagged as Observer because {{className}} exposes a virtual notification method (lines {{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}) of the update/notify family that a subject calls to fan a state change out to many dependents.

observer_virtual_marker.note: `virtual` — without it the subject's callback would bind statically and the fan-out to different observer types would not dispatch.

observer_update_method.note: The notification callback. The subject invokes this for every registered observer; each observer decides independently what to do with the change.

runnerUpComparisonTemplates.Strategy: Strategy also dispatches through a virtual interface, but it swaps one algorithm at a call site. Observer broadcasts a change to many registered listeners — the cardinality is one-to-many, not one-of-many.
runnerUpComparisonTemplates.Mediator: Mediator centralises interaction in a hub the components call. Observer pushes a change outward to subscribers that registered themselves; there is no central coordinator deciding who reacts.
